title: Ben Affleck Unites ’90s Sitcom Stars in Dunkin’s Chaotic and Nostalgic Super Bowl Ad
summary: Dunkin's nostalgic Super Bowl ad (“Good Will Dunkin’”) stars Ben Affleck, Jennifer Aniston, Matt LeBlanc, Jason Alexander and several other '90s sitcom greats.
story: The stars — specifically, sitcom stars — have aligned at Dunkin’ for its latest Super Bowl spectacle.

Dunkin’ just dropped its 2026 Super Bowl ad, after airing a teaser of Ben Affleck pitching a “cringe” VHS tape to fellow ’90s sitcom stars Jennifer Aniston, Matt LeBlanc and Jason Alexander the week prior.

In the minute-long commercial, Affleck reveals the contents of the tape he showed the trio in the teaser: a mashup of at least six sitcoms, several stars and one movie, in what he calls “Good Will Dunkin.’”
While last year’s batty, coffee bean-covered, DunKings-themed ad also starred Affleck, with cameos from Tom Brady, Matt Damon and Jeremy Strong, this year’s pivots from music to television.

It’s a never-aired sitcom pilot set in 1995 — the year the brand put iced coffee on its menu. The episode follows the accidental “invention” of iced coffee inside a Dunkin’ in Cambridge, Massachusetts, by a cast of familiar faces — nine of them, to be exact.
“Before the movie, a much better version of ‘Good Will Hunting’ was made as a sitcom with a real genius in the lead and some other actors,” Affleck says at the start of the commercial.
The ad imagines a small-screen version of the Oscar-winning film with Affleck — instead of Damon — as Will. And instead of doing janitorial work, Will is employed at a Dunkin’ by boss Alexander.
“Hey, Will, did you arrange the Munchkins in the Fibonacci sequence?” Alexander, known for his role as George Costanza on “Seinfeld,” asks. “I got a genius working for me.”
Nostalgic, cringeworthy sitcom tropes dominate the commerical, as actors recite iconic lines from “Friends” and other shows of the time.

Referencing a Costanza line, Jasmine Guy, who played Whitney Gilbert on “A Different World,” says, “I will marry the first man that can help me with the Fibonacci sequence,” and several men gather to help.
“‘A Different World’ will always be family to me, so stepping back into that era for Dunkin’ felt like coming home,” Guy tells TODAY.com. “Sharing the set with this cast made it a real highlight.”
Aniston appears at the end of the ad, after breaking up with Affleck’s character, to show off her new boyfriend.
“You like doughnuts?” Aniston asks.
“Yeah,” Affleck replies.
“Well, this is my new boyfriend,” Aniston says, pulling Brady into frame. She asks, “How do you like ‘deez nuts’?” in a reference to Dr. Dre’s 1992 song.
The cast shrugs, ending the ad but not the memory of the former Patriot’s salad-bowl-shaped wig, worthy of its own laugh track.
The corny commercial is full of gasp-worthy cameos from sitcom greats — including one 78-year-old who appears just as he did in the ’80s.
“Dunkin’ brought the right people together with such a funny idea, and the energy on set made it all click,” Ted Danson tells TODAY.com. “It was a really fun day.”
There is so much star power in this commercial, we thought a bulleted list of actors in order of appearance may be helpful:

Ben Affleck — never a sitcom star, but did appear once in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer.”Jason Alexander — George Costanza from “Seinfeld” Matt LeBlanc — Joey Tribbiani from “Friends”Jasmine Guy — Whitney Gilbert from “A Different World”Alfonso Ribeiro — Carlton Banks from “The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air”Jaleel White — Steve Urkel/Stefan Urquelle from “Family Matters”Ted Danson — Sam Malone from “Cheers”Jennifer Aniston — Rachel Green from “Friends”Tom Brady — never a sitcom star, but a football great in a wacky wig
Ben Affleck — never a sitcom star, but did appear once in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer.”
Jason Alexander — George Costanza from “Seinfeld”
Matt LeBlanc — Joey Tribbiani from “Friends”
Jasmine Guy — Whitney Gilbert from “A Different World”
Alfonso Ribeiro — Carlton Banks from “The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air”
Jaleel White — Steve Urkel/Stefan Urquelle from “Family Matters”
Ted Danson — Sam Malone from “Cheers”
Jennifer Aniston — Rachel Green from “Friends”
Tom Brady — never a sitcom star, but a football great in a wacky wig

title: Who Is Ann Michael Maye? All About Drake Maye's Wife
summary: Who is Drake Maye's wife, Ann Michael Maye? Here's what to know about Ann Michael Maye including her career as a food TikToker, her NBC show, her athletic career, her age and more.
story: From high school football games to Super Bowl LX, Patriots quarterback Drake Maye has had the same girl cheering on the sidelines for him.
The QB met his wife, Ann Michael Maye, formerly Ann Michael Hudson, all the way back in middle school.

Going into nearly two years of marriage, Drake Maye, 23, has an opportunity to claim another ring this year with hopes of winning this year’s Super Bowl, where the Patriots are set to face off against the Seattle Seahawks.

“Her watching my middle school football games playing quarterback to now getting a chance to watch me in the super bowl … I wouldn’t rather do it with anybody else,” Drake Maye said in a press conference.
At the big match, Ann Michael Maye, 22, will be cheering Drake Maye on.

“WE ARE GOING TO THE SUPER BOWL!!!!!” Ann Michael Maye wrote in the caption of an Instagram post shared after the Patriots’ AFC Championship win. “Words can’t even begin to describe how proud I am of you, #10!!!! Watching your dreams come true is the biggest blessing and I love you!!!!!”
Here’s what to know about Ann Michael Maye and her own formidable career.
What Ann Michael Maye Is Wearing to the Super Bowl
Ahead of the game, Ann Michael Maye posted a snippet of her Super Bowl weekend on Instagram, with a carousel that included fit checks, dinner out and a game of mahjong with her husband.
Then, photos of her with Drake Maye on game day show her wearing a vintage Patriots jacket and polka dot pants.
She shared a mirror selfie of her outfit on her Instagram story.
The Couple Shared a Sweet Kiss Before the Game Began
The couple was photographed sharing a kiss before the Super Bowl began.
She wrote on her Instagram story, “Proud is an understatement. I love you @drakemaye,” next to a photo of them kissing.
They Met in Middle School
The couple have been linked since middle school, according to a TikTok throwback Ann Michael Maye posted in 2024.
The couple celebrated their nine-year anniversary in 2024.
“9 years with you and it keeps getting better everyday❤️ I couldn’t imagine my life with out you as my best friend. I love you more than you know!” Ann Michael Maye wrote on Instagram.
Both Attended the University of North Carolina
After high school, Drake Maye went on to play football at University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill starting in 2021.
Ann Michael Maye also went to UNC, studying business administration with an entrepreneurship and conflict management double minor, according to her LinkedIn.
She was also the vice president of finance at her sorority, Kappa Delta. She also completed two strategy internships.
The Couple Married in 2025
Drake Maye got down on one knee with an ocean view and announced their engagement on Instagram on January 15, 2025.
“Love doing life with you. Can’t wait to marry you!” he wrote in a caption.
The couple exchanged vows later that year on June 21, according to Ann Michael Maye’s Instagram. The next day, they shared photos from the wedding on Instagram.
“What a night! Cheers to forever🥂I love you!” Drake Maye wrote.
Drake Maye Is Happy He Married Young
During a conference with the media ahead of the Super Bowl, Drake Maye shared the relationship advice he gives his Patriots teammates.

“My teammates have the right to whatever they choose but I would definitely advise them to eventually get married,” he said. “It’s one of the best things I’ve experienced in my life. There’s no better feeling than coming home to a wife that loves you and cares for you.”
“I know my teammates, some of them are experiencing different parts of their life. I chose to get married young, and I don’t regret it one bit,” he continued.
She’s an Athlete
Ann Michael Maye played sports in high school, including softball and basketball.
“She’s a great athlete,” Drake Maye said at a press conference. “She beats me at a lot of sports.”
Ann Michael Maye Is Now a Food Influencer
The 22-year-old has a large social media presence. She shares cooking and day-in-the-life content with over 500,000 followers on TikTok.
Leading up to Christmas of last year, she baked something different for 25 days as part of a challenge called Bakemas.

In early 2026, she debuted her NBC Sports Boston baking show, “Beyond Bakemas.” So far, she’s been joined by guests like Bartsool’s Dave Portnoy, former Patriot star David Andrews, and content creator Emma Pritchard.
In Episode 2, she disclosed which hungry football players have been especially appreciative of her baking skills.
“The O-line is the main recipient of my baked goods,” she said.

Ann Michael Maye said that the players will send her funny thank you notes for the sweet treats, with messages like, “We need to protect your husband so we need to keep on the extra weight,” and, “If you need a bodyguard, we’ll be your bodyguard.”
The famous cookie that’s been fueling the Patriots ahead of Super Bowl weekend is Ann Michael’s “Cinnamon Roll Snickerdoodles,” which Drake Maye has claimed to be his favorite.
Fans Absolutely Love Her
Drake Maye is not the only one obsessed with his wife, Ann Michael Maye is adored by the Patroit’s fandom. She’s been dubbed “Queen of the North” and “First Lady of New England.”
“I’m only watching the Super Bowl because Ann is watching the Super Bowl,” one fan commented on her TikTok.
Fans expressed why they can’t get enough of Ann Michael in a TODAY segment that aired Feb. 6.
“I just think she’s so adorable and so unapologetically herself,” Merrick Moore said.
“It’s kind of nice because she doesn’t make Drake her personality,” Madye Moore said.
In preparation for the big game on Sunday, there are even “I ❤️ Ann Michael” sweatshirts being sold.
Ann Michael Maye Cheers Drake Maye On in Style
Ann Michael Maye attends her husband’s games and isn’t shy about her fashion. She typically stunts in comfortable yet colorful and bold attire.
Most recently, she served at the AFC Championship game, wearing a retro collaged Patriot coat with bright red pants.
What She’s Packing for the Super Bowl
On Feb. 4, Ann Michael Maye posted a TikTok of what she’s packing to the Super Bowl, and it includes three suitcases.
She added a luggage tag that says, “Go Pats 10,” referencing her husband’s number.

In her carryon, she had needlepoint, a “Harry Potter” book, a travel-sized mahjong set, a devotion book and her journal and of course a Patriots Hat. On the way, she finished a needlepoint luggage tag and also made one for her husband that says, “I love Drake Maye.”
It’s Ann Michael, Not Ann
Drake Maye pointed out that his wife’s full first name is Ann Michael, not Ann.
“Her name’s Ann Michael, not just Ann. I don’t like people calling her Ann and she doesn’t like it either,” he said in a press conference.
She Has ‘Beef’ with Seahawks’ QB’s Fiancée, Katie Hoofnagle
Ann Michael Maye and Katie Hoofnagle, who is engaged to Seahawks QB Sam Darnold, dropped ads for DoorDash joking about “settling the beef” ahead of the Super Bowl.
The beef, in this case, refers to which of their football teams’ home cities has better food.
Hoofnagle said, in hers, “no one does it better than Seattle,” calling ramen “kind of like clam chowder, but better.” Ann Michael Maye said New England’s coffee “dunks“ on Seattle’s.
The two posed in photos for the ads, showing the beef is all imaginary.

title: Here’s Who’s Performing the National Anthem at the Super Bowl 2026
summary: Who is singing the national anthem at the Super Bowl? What to know about all the performers and who sings the Star Spangled Banner.
story: The Super Bowl is full of fanfare, with a highly anticipated showdown on the field and an epic halftime performance.
Kicking it all off is a special rendition of the national anthem, often sung by a major celebrity.
Every year, one musician is given the opportunity to sing the Star-Spangled Banner in front of thousands of Super Bowl attendees and millions of viewers at home.
Curious to know who will be belting it out on the football field this year? Here are all the details.
Who Is Singing the 2026 Super Bowl National Anthem?
Musician Charlie Puth, who is known for hits like “We Don’t Talk Anymore” and “Attention,” will be singing the national anthem at the 2026 Super Bowl.
In an interview with Rolling Stone, Puth referred to the national anthem as “the hardest song ever written.”
The 34-year-old recalled how he ended up applying for the gig.
“I auditioned for it, but I made up my own audition because I’ve always wanted to do it ­— because I love it musically. It’s the best song. Musically, it’s so special,” he said.
When asked if he will be singing live, Puth said, “I’ll be singing. The mic will be on.” He also noted that certain parts of the song are prerecorded for practicality.
“It’s impossible to mic an orchestra and expect that it’s gonna sound good in a stadium that’s filled with a hundred thousand people that are going to be cheering,” he said.
During the interview, Puth noted that he’s inspired by Whitney Houston, who sang the national anthem at the Super Bowl in 1991 and is also a fellow New Jersey native.

“It’s a great honor. I’m going to be inspired by what Whitney did, but I can’t ever touch what she did. That’s the best one ever done — that and the Chris Stapleton one. That was raw. Made grown men cry. I just want to do my own thing with the hardest piece of music ever written. And I just wanna show people that I can do it,” he said.
Who’s Performing the Halftime Show?

Puerto Rican superstar Bad Bunny is taking the stage at the Apple Music Super Bowl 60 halftime show. This is the musician’s first time as a Super Bowl halftime show headliner, but he previously appeared as a guest in 2020 during Shakira and Jennifer Lopez’s 2020 halftime show performance.
Who Else is Performing?
In addition to the national anthem and halftime show, Super Bowl attendees and viewers will also be treated to several other performances.
Teddy Swims will kick things off by performing at the Super Bowl Tailgate at 3:50 p.m. ET. LaRussell will be his opening act. The performance will be available to stream live on Peacock.
At 6 p.m. ET, the following artists will perform during the Super Bowl Opening Ceremony: Green Day, Charlie Puth, Brandi Carlile and Coco Jones.

title: What Time Is Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl Halftime Show?
summary: What time is the Super Bowl halftime show? Here's how to tune in to watch Bad Bunny's halftime show at Super Bowl 60.
story: In just a few hours, the New England Patriots and Seattle Seahawks will battle it out in Super Bowl 60. But there’s another major element that will likely attract even more eyes: Bad Bunny’s halftime show.

This year, the Puerto Rican superstar — fresh off winning the top honor at the Grammys last weekend — is headlining the Apple Music Super Bowl Halftime Show. Apple has sponsored the program since 2023 when Rihanna took the stage, followed by Usher in 2024 and Kendrick Lamar last year.

Whether you’re a football fanatic or a casual viewer, tuning into the halftime show has become a collective cultural moment, and this year’s show is likely to be just as iconic as those in years’ past.
Wondering what time the Super Bowl halftime show will start? Here’s everything you need to know.
Who’s Performing at the Super Bowl Halftime Show?
In September, music lovers learned that Bad Bunny would be headlining the 2026 Super Bowl halftime show.
The Puerto Rican superstar previously appeared as a guest during Shakira and Jennifer Lopez’s 2020 halftime show performance, but this will be his first time as its headline performer.
Who Will Bad Bunny Bring Out During His Halftime Show?
Super Bowl halftime show performers often bring special guests with them. For instance, SZA joined Kendrick Lamar on stage during his 2025 performance.
However, many musicians opt to keep the details of their performance under wraps and let viewers watch the action unfold live.
Ahead of Bad Bunny’s halftime show, there’s plenty of speculation over which musicians he might invite on stage.

The Hollywood Reporter noted that many names have been thrown out, including Jennifer Lopez, Shakira, Cardi B, Drake, J Balvin, Marc Anthony, El Gran Combo de Puerto Rico, Daddy Yankee, Rosalía, Travis Scott and Dua Lipa.
What Time Is the Super Bowl Halftime Show?
Super Bowl kick off is at 6:30 p.m. ET/ 3:30 p.m. PT on Sunday, Feb. 8, and the game consists of four 15-minute quarters.
As any football fan knows, viewers must also account for pauses during calls, time outs and commercial breaks. On average, the Super Bowl usually lasts three and a half hours.

Since the timing of each quarter is unpredictable, it’s hard to know what time the halftime show will start. However, the halftime show takes place at the midpoint of the game, so it’s likely to start sometime between 8-8:30 p.m. ET/ 5-5:30 p.m. PT.
How to Watch the Super Bowl Halftime Show

Viewers can tune into the 2026 Super Bowl exclusively on NBC. The big game will air live at 6:30 p.m. ET/ 3:30 p.m. PT on Sunday, Feb. 8. Peacock subscribers can also watch the Super Bowl on the streaming service.
There are a few other ways to tune in:

title: Who Is Sam Darnold's Future Wife? All About the Seahawks QB's Fiancée, Katie Hoofnagle
summary: Who is Sam Darnold's Fiancee, Katie Hoofnagle? Here's what to know about her age, photos and more.
story: The Seattle Seahawks' star quarterback has found his forever cheerleader in Katie Hoofnagle.

The pair hard-launched their relationship in June 2023 and have remained relatively private since. While it's unclear when they started dating, Sam Darnold and Hoofnagle revealed their engagement in a July 2025 Instagram post.
Darnold spoke about his fiancée's support amid preparations for Super Bowl 60, where he and the Seahawks will face Drake Maye and the New England Patriots in a battle for the coveted Lombardi trophy.
Addressing reporters on Super Bowl 60 opening night Feb. 2, Darnold was asked about how he keeps his peace during the season.
"I think it comes back to, for me, having a great fiancée. She does a lot for me, and she keeps me calm," Darnold said. "She reminds me of why I do what I do, why I go to work every single day."
Read on for more about Darnold and Hoofnagle's relationship ahead of the 2026 Super Bowl.
She Grew up Around San Francisco, California
Super Bowl 60 marks a homecoming for Hoofnagle, who attended the Bay Area's Monte Vista High School, where she was captain of the varsity soccer team, according to posts from her Instagram account.
She Attended the University of South Carolina
Hoofnagle went on to attend the University of South Carolina, where she continued to play soccer, per her Instagram.
After graduation, she lived in Charlotte, North Carolina, often posting photos from Bank of America Stadium.
Of note, Darnold played on the Carolina Panthers from 2021-2022,  a year before he and Hoofnagle hard-launched their relationship on Instagram.
The Pair Went Public with Their Relationship in June 2023
In a post celebrating Darnold's birthday, Hoofnagle hard-launched her relationship status with the football star.
The carousel featured pictures of the pair smiling together at an event for the Jessie Rees Foundation, a nonprofit Darnold supports that provides aid to pediatric cancer patients.
"your huge heart, goofy energy, and ability to quote every movie you’ve ever seen are unmatched. happy 26th birthday, celebrating you is so easy! 🤍" she wrote in the caption.
She’s Supported His Football Career Through Several Moves
Since his first season in the NFL in 2018, Darnold has played for five different teams.
Darnold was first drafted by the New York Jets before being traded to the Carolina Panthers, which he played for from 2021 to 2022

In 2023, Darnold played for the San Francisco 49ers as backup quarterback to Brock Purdy. After debuting their relationship, Hoofnagle posted in support of Darnold after the 49ers won the NFC championship in January 2024.

The next season, Darnold played for the Minnesota Vikings, and Hoofnagle celebrated the move with a post to Instagram. She captioned the post, "here’s to the next chapter — SKOL!" referring to the team's signature chant.
After the Vikings won a tight game at the end of their season, Hoofnagle posted a picture to Instagram celebrating the victory.

Darnold now plays for the Seattle Seahawks and has made it to the Super Bowl as their star quarterback. Hoofnagle posted a series of images after the Seahawks won the NFC championship on Jan. 25, including a picture of her embracing her fiancée. She captioned the post, "WE’RE NOT DONE YET!!!"
She’s Friends with Ann Michael Maye, Wife of Patriots QB Drake Maye
Based on her Instagram, Hoofnagle is friends with Ann Michael Maye, the wife of the New England Patriots' quarterback, Drake Maye.
Ann Michael Maye commented on Hoofnagle’s engagement announcement in July 2025, writing “Congratulations!!! So excited for you guys🤍.”
They also previously posted a photo together in January 2025, seemingly hanging out before the Super Bowl in New Orleans.
Now, their respective significant others are about to go head-to-head in the league's biggest game.
Hoofnagle and Darnold Made Their Red Carpet Debut at the 2025 NFL Honors
The couple posed together on the red carpet for the NFL Honors in 2025. Hoofnagle wore a stunning sparkling dress that cinched at the waist.
The Couple Got Engaged in July 2025
Hoofnagle shared a series of photos from their engagement to Instagram.
"YES!!!!!" she captioned the original post.
A few days later, she posted more pictures to celebrate the occasion. These pictures were from an engagement photoshoot on a beach in Dana Point, California.
"a couple more from the best day ever🕊️ here’s to forever together," she wrote.

title: Cuteness Overload! How to Watch the 2026 Puppy Bowl
summary: The annual Puppy Bowl returns for a rematch between Team Ruff and Team Fluff, a couple of hours before Super Bowl LX. Find out when, what channel and time the 2026 Puppy Bowl happens.
story: Catch these pups ruffing around on Sunday.
The annual Puppy Bowl is set for yet another rematch between Team Ruff and Team Fluff, a couple of hours before 2026 Super Bowl.

The Seattle Seahawks will face the New England Patriots at Levi’s Stadium in Santa Clara, California, for the Super Bowl on Sunday — and it isn't a traditional Super Bowl paw-ty without the Puppy Bowl.

“Puppy Bowl XXII celebrates the spirit of the underdog with a gameday celebration that combines the excitement of a sports matchup with uplifting adoption stories,” Joseph Boyle, Discovery Channel's head of content, said in a press release.
The 22nd annual Puppy Bowl also marks new records. This year, 150 dogs from 72 shelters across the U.S., Puerto Rico, and the British Virgin Islands are featured, and these puppies are ready to roll.
Leading the game will be Dan Schachner, who is returning for his 15th year as referee. Providing the play-by-play for the event are sports broadcasters Steve Levy and Taylor Rooks.
Here’s everything to know about Puppy Bowl 2026.
What Time Is the Puppy Bowl 2026?
This year's Puppy Bowl is on Sunday, Feb. 8  at 2 p.m. ET and concludes at 5 p.m. ET.
How to Watch Puppy Bowl 2026 on TV
The Puppy Bowl will air on Animal Planet, Discovery, TBS and truTV.
An hour before kickoff, a Puppy Bowl XXII Kickoff Show will start at 1 p.m. ET.
How to Watch the Puppy Bowl Online
The 22nd annual Puppy Bowl is also available on HBO Max and Discovery+ for live streaming of these furry friends.
Who’s Competing in the Puppy Bowl?
With a record-breaking 150 dog participants, this year's matchup is set to be paw-some.

Team Fluff returns as the reigning champions to face Team Ruff.  Last year, Team Fluff secured the win down to the final minute with an end score of 68-66. If Team Fluff wins this year, it would be a back-to-back win.
At the end of the game, the Most Valuable Puppy (MVP) and Subaru of America, Inc. Underdog Award will be awarded to two outstanding players.
Puppy love is not the only energy in store for this year. The Puppy Bowl is also debuting a Team Oldies versus Team Goldies game, a senior dog special exhibition game.
Keeping up with tradition, the 150 dogs from 72 shelters across the U.S., Puerto Rico, and the British Virgin Islands are looking for a loving home and will be available for adoption.
Some of the cutest fur babies are featured on this year's lineup, with stars like Paws Allen, Dee-bone Samuel, and bark Kent.

title: Bad Bunny’s ‘Tití Me Preguntó’ Gets Everyone Dancing, But What’s It About?
summary: Bad Bunny is set to take on one of the biggest stages in the world when he headlines the 2026 Super Bowl halftime show
story: Bad Bunny is set to take on one of the biggest stages in the world when he headlines the 2026 Super Bowl halftime show.
The Puerto Rican superstar recently made history at the Grammys for taking home the album of the year trophy for “Debí Tirar Más Fotos,” the first Spanish-language album to win the category.

This Sunday, Feb. 8, he will rock the halftime show as the first male Latino artist to headline the major music event when the New England Patriots take on the Seattle Seahawks at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California, during the 2026 Super Bowl.
Bad Bunny is expected to sing many of his hits, including “DtMF” from his Grammy-winning studio album and “Tití Me Preguntó.”

The mega-hit was featured on the Puerto Rican singer’s fifth studio album, “Un Verano Sin Ti,” along with his popular track “Moscow Mule.” “Tití Me Preguntó”s catchy beat and lyrics made it Bad Bunny’s second-highest charting track from the album on the Billboard Hot 100.

Want to know what “Tití Me Preguntó” is all about? The track is a response to an imaginary conversation. The singer’s aunt — “Tití” — asks him if he has a lot of girlfriends, to which he replies that he does. He says that he would like to fall in love but can’t due to his trust issues.
The song became a worldwide hit, with Time Magazine even naming it the 2022 song of the year. The accompanying music video was shot in New York, celebrating the Puerto Rican community and people.
Ahead of Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl halftime show, we’re translating the lyrics.

title: Nick Jonas' New Album Cover for 'Sunday Best' Honors His Diabetes Diagnosis
summary: Nick Jonas talks to TODAY.com about his new album "Sunday Best," how he honors his diabetes diagnosis and which song is dedicated to his daughter.
story: For over 20 years, Nick Jonas has drawn inspiration from his life for his music, including his Type 1 diabetes diagnosis.

The singer and one-third of the Jonas Brothers was diagnosed with the chronic condition at 13. To express his feelings about his diagnosis, he put pen to paper, resulting in the emotional and reflective song “A Little Bit Longer” — included in the Jonas Brothers’ 2008 album of the same name.
Now age 33, Jonas has again been inspired by his health journey, highlighting his continuous glucose monitor (CGM) on the cover of his new album, “Sunday Best.”

“This album for me was all about transparency. I worked with some incredible co-writers who really helped me articulate some things that I’ve been thinking and feeling for a long time,” Jonas tells TODAY.com. “The fact that the music is some of my most transparent and vulnerable that I’ve ever written, I wanted the album cover to symbolize, first of all, this idea of looking at yourself in the mirror.”
Jonas says there are moments when he and others ask themselves: “Are we being the best version of ourselves today? Are we being nice to ourselves today?”

“And that’s why I wanted that reflective moment,” he explains. “When I looked at the frame and saw the Dexcom G7 on my arm, I was really excited at the idea of leaning in and not doing a version without it because I want people to see this is the real me. This is what my life is like, day in, day out, living with Type 1 diabetes.”

Type 1 diabetes occurs when the pancreas doesn't make enough insulin, causing glucose to build up in the bloodstream, which can lead to a range of complications, according to the American Diabetes Association. The condition is usually diagnosed in kids but can develop at any time, and doctors don't know why. There's no cure, and the primary treatment is taking insulin.
“(Diabetes) is a part of my story. It’s part of my DNA at this point,” Jonas says.

“I’m really going to go to very honest places with this (new) music and (speaking) about a number of things ... my relationship, fatherhood, my experiences, and living with Type 1 diabetes for over 20 years is a huge part of my story," he says.
Below, Jonas dives deeper into how he manages his Type 1 diabetes, the most personal songs on “Sunday Best,” and what he’s most looking forward to in 2026.
This interview has been edited and condensed for clarity.
How Do You Manage Your Diabetes Day to Day?

People living with Type 1 diabetes have to make hundreds of decisions a day to better manage their disease, little things like when you need a snack, or adjusting your insulin delivery, just watching the trends of your glucose. These are all things that add extra layers to an already very busy life and hectic life.

What I try to do is make that next best decision and then, obviously, staying active and getting your body moving is always really helpful. When you’re on tour, it’s tough to find time sometimes, so even just a nice, long walk can do.

I think that there's a growing conversation around the mental and emotional health aspects of living with diabetes, and things like diabetes burnout. It's been great to see that awareness continue to grow and to be a small part of that conversation.
Which Songs on Your New Album, 'Sunday Best,' Reference Your Health Journey?

The first song of the album is called "Sweet to Me," and it is about a lot of different things, but the first verse begins talking about the safety and innocence of childhood — memories like going to Blockbuster with my dad and the hot pizza box on my lap, and how visceral that feeling of just knowing that you're safe to walk around your neighborhood and everyone knows each other's names, and wanting to get back in touch with those simpler days.
As the song progresses, the second verse touches on a lyric, which I love: "When the doctor told me there's too much sugar in your bloodstream, baby, that's too sweet. It's just too sweet."

When I wrote "A Little Bit Longer," it was this more negative and harder to comprehend side of living with Type 1. But now I have a real appreciation for it and the experiences I've had in my work with Beyond Type 1, my nonprofit, but also meeting people in this community and being able to use my platform as a way to speak about something that's incredibly important to me.

It has given me a lot of wonderful, life-changing memories, and it's really nice to see that contrast in my songwriting, too. I have a wonderful wife and a 4-year-old daughter, and I just see everything through the lens of how grateful I am to have my family by my side and still be doing what I’m doing. It’s not to say that there aren’t tough days, but I see everything through that lens of how the song says, “It’s just so sweet to me.”
With the Single 'Gut Punch,' Why Was It Important for You to Be So Vulnerable?
It’s probably easy for some people to look at my life and think that I never have thoughts like that, but that’s just fundamentally not true. The themes in this album are universal.

You don’t have to have lived my life to understand what it’s like to look at yourself in the mirror and question yourself, (have) self-doubt, negative self-talk. I’ve loved hearing from people about how the song has been healing for them and seeing online people connecting to the bridge. It’s about getting back in touch with your inner child.
How Do You Manage Your Diabetes on Tour?

Having access to the frequency of the glucose readings that I get really helps. I saw a major change the minute I got on CGM. As the technology has gotten better, the ability to integrate into the smartphone has been really helpful.

In addition, it's about just trying my best each day to handle it with as much care as I can. Also knowing that there are going to be some days that are tough and unpredictable, and to not get stressed about that and to be upfront with the people you love in your inner circle about, "Hey, my glucose is high or it's low." Not even saying, I need you to do anything, but just to alert them so that they're aware in case something comes up.
What Are Your Go-To Snacks for Managing Your Blood Sugar?

If it’s low, Welch’s Fruit Snacks are always good. Generally, I eat a pretty low-carb diet, some good protein, macronutrients and then a sustaining carb, something that helps if I’m going on the stage, a good baseline. But also it’s difficult when you’re on the road, there’s not a lot of choices. Sometimes you just have to improvise and figure it out.
What Are You Looking Forward to in 2026?

I join my “Jumanji” cast mates again pretty soon, which will be fun, getting back on the “Jumanji” set. There’s another film project, which is coming together right now and aiming to start production fairly soon, which would be great because it’s a project I’ve been developing for a while, and the development process can be very long and exhausting, so when you finally get to a start date, it's very exciting. It's top secret, but there'll be an announcement about it soon. And a lot of shows, both with the brothers and solo throughout the rest of the year.

title: Why Robert Irwin Is Rooting for the Seahawks in the Super Bowl
summary: Robert Irwin says he's rooting for the Seattle Seahawks in Super Bowl 60 because of his mother's ties to the region.
story: It somehow seems fitting that Robert Irwin would be rooting for a hawk — a Seahawk, that is.
The conservationist, who is from Australia, says he is pulling for the Seattle Seahawks to beat the New England Patriots in Super Bowl 60.
“I don’t think people realize just how huge the NFL is in Australia,” he told TODAY.com at the NFL Honors in San Francisco on Feb. 6.
“I grew up watching the Super Bowl because my mom is from Oregon, Pacific Northwest. So I got to back Seattle. I love my Pacific Northwesters.”
The "Dancing with the Stars" champ also has an affinity for the Seahawks because their punter, Michael Dickson, is from Down Under.
“They’ve also got an Australian in their midst. The punter, he’s in there. So I think they’re going to take it out this year,” he said.

Irwin is further psyched that the NFL will continue to expand its schedule outside of the United States by holding its first-ever game in Australia next season when the San Francisco 49ers and Los Angeles Rams play in Melbourne.
No date has yet been finalized, but Irwin is all in on how the Super Bowl unites people.
“I love the vibe. I love the energy. I love the community because just walking around, I keep running into people that I know,” he said.
“It’s amazing how many different people it brings together. And we’ve got a game coming to Australia this year, so I’m proud to represent a little bit of Australia on the carpet tonight.”
Super Bowl 60 takes place Feb. 8 at 6:30. The game will be broadcast live on NBC and streamed on Peacock.

title: ‘The Traitors’ Season 4, Episode 8 Recap: Who Was Banished and Who Was Murdered?
summary: In Episode 8, viewers learned if the Traitors were able to temporarily put aside their differences and continue working together. Read on for a recap of Episode 8, below.
story: Warning: Spoilers for Season 4, Episode 8 of “The Traitors” below…
As the end of the game draws closer, the Season 4 Traitors are struggling to keep their identities a secret.

Last week on “The Traitors,” “The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills” alum and original Traitor Lisa Rinna was banished by the group partly due to “Love Island USA” Season 6 star and fellow Traitor Rob Rausch turning on her.

Then, “The Real Housewives of Potomac” star Candiace Dillard Bassett, the third Traitor, countered by casting a vote for Rausch at the Round Table. The episode ended with Rausch and Dillard Bassett meeting in the turret. The two were clearly no longer united in their mission to deceive the Faithfuls, with Rausch’s treacherous move shattering the trust between him and Dillard Bassett.
In Episode 8, which was released on Peacock Thursday, Feb. 5, viewers learned if the Traitors were able to temporarily put aside their differences and continue working together.
So, did they evade suspicion after committing the next murder? Or, did the Faithfuls successfully banish another Traitor? Read on for a recap of Episode 8, below.
Candiace and Rob Go Head-to-Head in the Turret
The episode opens with Rausch attempting to explain to Dillard Bassett why he turned on Rinna.
“As a person, I feel bad. But as a player, Lisa had to go,” he says.

Dillard Bassett shares in her confessional that Rausch is a threat to her, but she has to appear unbothered or else he will stab her in the back. So, she attempts to move past Rinna’s banishment and also tells Rausch that her burn vote against him was meaningless.

Host Alan Cumming then enters the turret and informs the Traitors that they can either commit a murder or recruit a Faithful to their team. Rausch and Dillard Bassett agree that they want to remain a duo before discussing who is on the chopping block. But Dillard Bassett is secretly plotting her next move.
“I need to make Rob feel like he is still a part of a team…a careful assassin serves her revenge cold,” she says in her confessional.
A Calculated Murder

At breakfast, the players who arrive first chat about Rinna’s banishment. Maura Higgins, from the UK version of “Love Island,” notes that it was odd Dillard Bassett chose to vote for Rausch despite an overwhelming majority choosing to banish Rinna. After all the remaining players enter the breakfast room, viewers learn that Dillard Bassett and Rausch chose to murder “The Bachelor” star Colton Underwood.

We flash back to the turret where Dillard Bassett is the one to suggest murdering Underwood. In his final confessional, the former “Bachelor” lead says, “Well that’s a bummer. Damn. I think it has become pretty obvious that Candiace is up there. Trying to be consistent and then do a throw away vote is sloppy gameplay.”
He ironically adds, “I have faith that the Faithfuls know who it is and I hope that my boy Rob wins.”

Dillard Bassett explains to viewers that she wanted to murder Underwood to weaken Rausch. However, the “Love Island USA” star is a step ahead of her. He paraphrases a Mark Twain quote and says, “It ain’t the things you don’t know that hurt you. It’s the things you think you know for sure,” before pointing out that Underwood had mentioned he was suspicious of Dillard Bassett before he was murdered. He withheld this information from Dillard Bassett.
A Picture-Perfect Mission

For the mission, the players have to split themselves into two teams. The team with eight players will be tasked with re-creating portraits based on descriptions written on plaques in the castle. A photo of their re-creation will be sent to an old gallery where the three remaining players will have to sort through a room of paintings and figure out which one matches the picture.

“Dancing With the Stars” pro Mark Ballas, “Top Chef” host Kristen Kish and Rausch are the three who go to the old gallery, which means they also have an opportunity to earn a shield. The group adds $12,000 to the prize pot and Kish finds the shield.
Candiace Calls Out Rob

Back at the castle, Dillard Bassett and “One Tree Hill” star Stephen Colletti’s names are being thrown out. Colletti tells Dillard Bassett about Underwood thinking she was a Traitor before he was murdered, which tips her off that Rausch set her up.

Rausch interrupts Dillard Bassett and Colletti’s conversation and asks to speak to her privately. When she asks him if he has been mentioning her name around the castle, he lies. She then tells him she thinks they have “different values.”
“I feel like you’re taking this very personally,” he replies before reminding her they are playing a game.
Dillard Bassett retorts, “It is a game — and you are a snake.” She warns him that if she gets banished then she will sabotage him on her way out.
Traitors Battle at the Round Table

Olympic figure skater Johnny Weir opens up the discussion at the Round Table by listing the reasons why he suspects Colletti is a Traitor. “The Real Housewives of New York City” alum Dorinda Medley chimes in to add some evidence she believes also points to Colletti being a Traitor. Then, singer Eric Nam mentions Dillard Bassett’s vote the previous week. She reacts by calling it a throwaway vote, but then says she now wonders if Rausch is a Traitor. She recalls him being nervous at breakfast. She also says Rausch was using Underwood to get Rinna out of the game.

After a brief, calm back-and-forth between Dillard Bassett and Rausch, it is time to vote. All the players, except Dillard Bassett and “Survivor” winner Natalie Anderson (who votes for Olympic figure skater Tara Lipinski), vote to banish “The Real Housewives of Potomac” alum.

While standing on the Circle of Truth, Dillard Bassett holds back tears as she says, “I don’t want to cry. I love you all so much. This experience has been life changing. I’ve never been in a situation where I’ve connected with so many people so quickly. I feel like I’ve played this game with valor, with love, with respect… And with treachery because I am a Traitor!”
During her final confessional, Dillard Bassett is in good spirits.

“I am proud of how I played this game. It was really challenging to have to lie. I really started to love everybody. And that’s what just I think stings the most,” she says. “But I’m confident that they’re going to look at Rob. And in the event that they don’t, joke is on them. So good luck to you all.”
A New Traitor Is Recruited

As the last Traitor in the game, Rausch realizes that the other players might start to suspect him and use Dillard Bassett’s previous vote as evidence. He enters the turret alone and Cumming tells him that “per the rules” he must give one Faithful an ultimatum: join him as a Traitor or die.
Rausch goes through the pros and cons of a few players before choosing Nam to be the next Traitor. When Nam receives his recruitment letter, he isn’t pleased.
“I’m almost there. I just want to be left alone,” he says about making it to the end of the game as a Faithful.
Nam leaves the castle and walks to the front steps where he is stunned to see Rausch reveal himself as a Traitor.
In Episode 9, out Feb. 12, viewers will learn if Nam accepts the offer, and if he and Rausch can survive until the end of the game.

title: 'Lincoln Lawyer' Season 4 Ending: Who Killed Sam, Does Mickey Get Out of Jail and More Questions Answered
summary: The ending of Lincoln Lawyer Season 4 explained, including who killed Sam and if Mickey gets out of jail. Find out here.
story: Spoilers ahead for Season 4 of “The Lincoln Lawyer.”
Season 4 of “The Lincoln Lawyer,” which premiered Feb. 4, might just be the heaviest and most emotional yet.

After the Season 3 cliffhanger, which saw Mickey Haller (Manuel Garcia-Rulfo) detained after his former client Sam Scales (Christopher Thornton) was found dead in his trunk, Mickey finds himself behind bars and the prime suspect in the death.

While Mickey heads to court, the rest of his team is overwhelmed with keeping their firm afloat and attempting to solve who is behind Sam's death. Then, there's the sudden and unexpected death of beloved mentor Legal Siegel (Elliott Gould), as well as Mickey and Maggie's (Neve Campbell) daughter Hayley (Krista Warner) being bullied at school.
As the episodes continue, it appears as if the FBI and police department are out to get Mickey, but there’s a bigger scheme at hand.
So what happens at the end of “The Lincoln Lawyer” Season 4 and who really killed Sam? Read on to find out.
What Happens at the End of 'The Lincoln Lawyer' Season 4?
Mickey's ex-wife Maggie, who is a criminal prosecutor, switches to the defense side to help him with his case.
The two are up against Dana “Death Row Dana” Berg (Constance Zimmer), who is one tough prosecutor and thinks Mickey is guilty.
Mickey maintains his innocence and believes he is being framed — and he is, but it's a little tricky.
His team discovers that Sam was involved in a scheme and betrayed another con artist for personal gain.

While investigating, they find that Sam had retrofitted a semi-truck and got a commercial driver’s license under a fake name so he could get a job at a biofuel company that was also running their own scam against the government.

Mickey asks Cisco (Angus Sampson) to track down Alex Gazarian, formerly Alex Grant (Michael A. Goorjian), to be a witness in his case. They believe he might have information on who is trying to frame Mickey.

Alex played a role in Mickey winning lover-turned-client Lisa Trammell's (Lana Parrilla) murder trial in Season 2. After she was accused of murdering real estate developer Mitchell Bondurant, Mickey won his case by making it seem as if construction company owner Alex was behind it.

Alex was then investigated by the FBI for his ties to the Armenian mob. The attention he received from the trial made him angry towards Mickey. In Season 4, a group of Armenian mobsters jump Cisco, which makes Mickey and his team think they might be behind Sam's death and trying to get back at him.

However, as Cisco attempts to subpoena Alex, he is killed by the same men who previously jumped him. Their only chance at finding out what happened to Sam is having Alex's girlfriend, Jeanine Ferrigno (Emmanuelle Chriqui), testify in court.
Jeanine, however, is afraid for her life and refuses to speak in court.
Who Killed Sam and Why?
It turns out that Sam and Alex were working together to defraud the government.

According to Mickey, the only people who could confirm their dealings were FBI Agent Ruth (Sasha Alexander) and Agent Vasquez (Hemky Madera), who previously threatened Mickey at his home. The agents had warned him about sticking his nose where it didn't belong and told him they would never take the stand. But why?
The federal agents are working on an active investigation that involved Alex, Sam and the biofuel companies that are defrauding the government.
The FBI is aware of about a dozen of refineries that are scamming the government by selling the same feedstock or cooking oil over and over again by changing the labels.
“And every time, they bill the government all over again for it,” Agent Ruth tells Mickey. “The players behind the scam are all the same too.”

She tells them that this is where Sam came into the picture. The FBI had hired Sam to be their inside man. Mickey blames the FBI for Sam’s death, suggesting that Alex had found out Sam was working for the feds.
However, that's not the case. Jeanine confesses to Maggie and Cisco that Alex had no idea Sam was working with the FBI.
Alex killed Sam because he found out that Sam was stealing from him.
Alex put Sam in Mickey’s trunk because he was mad at him for making him look guilty when he pleaded the fifth in Lisa’s trial. Because of that trial, Alex lost his construction company.
His family then turned on him because of the missing money and the attention he received from the FBI after the trial.
How Was Mickey Cleared of Sam's Death?
Jeanine refuses to testify but Mickey and his team devise a plan to scare the FBI into thinking that she will speak up and ruin their whole investigation.
While in court, FBI agents show up and tell the judge that Jeanine cannot testify. In exchange for her silence, they are willing to give her protection and offer to drop the charges against Mickey.

Mickey, however, has some conditions before he accepts the dismissed charges. He asks for a public statement from the DA’s office that completely exonerates him of any crime — and he wants Dana to deliver it.
“She is the one calling me a murderer for the last four months. She’s the one the world needs to hear it from,” he says, making a point to get his reputation back and prove he's innocent.
Mickey also asks for there to be an investigation into Officer Roy Collins, who pulled him over and framed him for Sam’s murder.
A Shocking New Discovery
All seems right in Mickey’s world after he is cleared of the murder charges. But in true “Lincoln Lawyer” fashion, something always has to come up.
While he’s at the grocery store, he keeps getting glances from a mystery woman, portrayed by Cobie Smulders. She goes up to him in the parking lot and then tells him to “get down!”
Shots are fired, cars crash and the FBI is at the scene. Agent Ruth appears and tells Mickey they have been following him because they had reason to believe he was still being targeted.
Dead, on the ground, is one of the Armenian mobsters who was about to shoot Mickey before the FBI agents killed him.
Thanks to the mystery woman, he is alive. When he asks who she is, she replies, “I’m your sister.”
And the season ends.
Will There Be a Season 5 of 'The Lincoln Lawyer'?
Yes, ahead of its Season 4 premiere, “The Lincoln Lawyer” was renewed for a fifth season.
According to the show's creator Ted Humphrey and co-showrunner Dailyn Rodriguez, they are “already hard at work on the next one,” per an interview with Tudum published Jan. 29.
“Season 4 is the most challenging and intensely personal journey we’ve taken Mickey Haller on yet, and we’re thrilled and grateful to be able to continue the ride in Season 5!” they added.

title: 1 Man's Quest to Learn to Dougie Is Bringing the Internet Together
summary: JB Star started a daily posting challenge where he tries to learn to Dougie. He's gone viral and gotten a bevy of adoring followers.
story: The year is 2010. Cali Swag District’s “Teach Me How to Dougie” is blasting from radio speakers and into iPods headphones, dominating school dances and house parties alike. Everyone is attempting the signature shoulder-lean move. Some, successfully. Most, not so much.
Sixteen years after the song came out, one man has decided it’s finally time to answer that call. Better late than never, right?
The Dougie, a dance move characterized by its signature shoulder lean and smooth swaying motion, became a cultural phenomenon in the early 2010s.
Jun Bae (JB) Star, a South Korea-based breakdancer who has been documenting his daily attempts to master the Dougie on TikTok and Instagram, has become an unexpected viral sensation.

With each video racking up hundreds of thousands, if not millions, of views, his 42-day (and counting) series proves that watching someone struggle through a decade-old dance move is exactly what the internet needed right now.
“I’ve always wanted to do the Dougie because I didn’t know how,” Star tells TODAY.com. “It was trending back in the 2010s, and I thought it would be a shame if I can’t do the Dougie in my life.”

What started as a modest daily posting challenge in December 2025 quickly transformed into something Star never anticipated. For the first week, his videos received minimal engagement. Then, while traveling internationally for a friend’s wedding, everything changed.
“I was posting at the hotel because it was a daily challenge,” he recalls. “I remember when I was at the wedding, it started blowing up.”
The Day 1 video, an earnest attempt based solely on Star’s mental image of what the Dougie should look like, became social media gold.
“It just looked so… ‘This guy can’t do it at all,’ so it’s kind of funny,” he recalls, laughing. “But that was really just me trying.”

As the days progressed, so did Star’s skills. The videos start with a warmup before Star attempts that day's Dougie. He switches up which version of the song he dances to, sometimes choosing the viral Enya mashup.

Viewers have watched his growth day by day, celebrating small victories and genuine effort as he works toward mastering the dance’s smooth, swaggering essence. The imperfect journey is exactly what has resonated with millions.
The comment sections of the videos have become their own ecosystem, with viewers bonding over their shared investment in JB’s progress.
One comment reads, "Well you may never get the dance down but you make THOUSANDS of people smile everyday so thanks for that!"
"Who do I feel so proud?" another commenter wrote on the Day 39 video.

Others make specific observations about his progress: "Your hips are finally nice and loose and feeling the rhythm! This is the second day in a row I’ve noticed that and I think that’s the biggest win!"
People are invested, to say the least. "I talked to my therapist about your progress this week," another wrote.
Star is well aware of the comments.
“There’s definitely a community that’s built around this series,” Star reflects. “Every morning I wake up, I look at the comments, and that makes me happy because people are engaging genuinely.”
For Star, who has created content for years, the response to this particular series has been unlike anything he’s experienced.

“Some people message me and say, ‘I’m going through a really hard time and this series is something that I look forward to every morning or every day,’” he shares. “Some messages have really impacted me as well. It’s getting bigger than me — the series is more than just me doing these funny videos.”
Star thinks that in a world grappling with political division and the eerie perfection of AI-generated content, his earnest, unfiltered journey offers something rare: genuine human imperfection.
“People see so much negative news or whatever they see that doesn’t necessarily make them feel good,” Star says. “People want to see something more lighthearted, something funny or comedic.”
He also believes the appeal lies in transparency. “What you’re seeing is just me pressing play and recording me learning the dance. It’s simple, straightforward, and easily recognizable,” he says.

Not everyone believes Star’s Dougie journey is authentic. Since the beginning of his social media experiment, skeptics have questioned whether the breakdancer is simply pretending not to know how to Dougie. Some have even accused him of being a member of the Jabbawockeez, the famous masked dance crew.
But Star insists his struggle is genuine. “I’m really not faking it, genuinely just trying my best. I think that’s what resonates with the audience as well,” he says.

The vast majority of his community agrees. Dance creators have offered to teach him via video calls. People from Dallas, Texas — where the Dougie originated — have invited him to learn from the architects themselves.
And perhaps most significantly, a member of Cali Swag District, the group behind the iconic “Teach Me How to Dougie” sensation, reached out to collaborate.

As for when the series will end? Star says he's not sure. As a self-described slow learner who took “years” to become proficient at breakdancing, he expects the Dougie journey to continue for quite a while.
But beyond the technical mastery of a viral dance move, Star has discovered something more profound: the unifying power of art and authentic self-expression.

“There’s so much politics and news that’s dividing people,” he says. “Dance has no borders and everyone can unite just through this simple Dougie dance. I’m learning every day and growing, not just the Dougie dance, but as a human as well through all these interactions.”

title: 'The Lincoln Lawyer' Kills Off a Beloved Character in Season 4. Who Dies and How?
summary: Who dies in Season 4 of The Lincoln Lawyer? Here's what happens to Legal Siegal and how Mickey Haller reacts to his death.
story: Spoilers ahead for Season 4 of “The Lincoln Lawyer.”
“The Lincoln Lawyer” revolves around the deaths and murders that defense attorney Mickey Haller successfully handles. But the latest death in Season 4 of the Netflix legal drama hits home.

The unexpected death comes midseason as Mickey (Manuel Garcia-Rulfo) himself is involved in the murder case involving his former client Sam Scales (Christopher Thornton), who at the end of Season 3 was found in the trunk of his car.

With the stress of Mickey's own legal troubles and future, Lorna (Becki Newton) takes on more cases to help their firm stay afloat and Maggie (Neve Campbell) even steps up by leaving her role as a criminal prosecutor to defend her ex-husband in court.
So it’s a shock when, in the show’s sixth episode of Season 4, a beloved character dies.
Who Dies in Season 4 of 'The Lincoln Lawyer'?
Sadly, David "Legal" Siegel  (Elliott Gould) suffers a heart attack at the end of Episode 6, breaking the hearts of Mickey, Lorna and the rest of the team who loved him.
The episode shows Legal at Mickey's office after he ditches the field trip his assisted living facility planned to the Broad so he could have lunch with the lawyer.
Since Mickey isn't there, Lorna offers to go to lunch with him in hopes that he'll give her advice on a difficult case she's working on.
The two then grab sandwiches — a No. 19 pastrami with Russian dressing for Legal, to be exact — at Langer's Deli.
Lorna and Legal bond over practicing law, with Legal sharing how his children didn't seem interested in his career while Mickey always asked questions and his advice.
Legal has always been a father figure and mentor to Mickey over the years, which Lorna acknowledges. They both express how worried they are about Mickey.
Later on, Legal is seen riding the bus when Lorna calls him to share an update on her case. He congratulates her on a job well done and gives her some words of wisdom.
As Mickey and Maggie deal with his case at the courthouse, Lorna gets a call that leaves her speechless and emotional. When she runs into Mickey and Maggie, she reveals that Legal had a heart attack.
“He was on the bus, and we were talking, I thought everything was fine ... He’s gone, Mickey,” Lorna says. “I’m so sorry. He’s gone.”
Lorna adds that Legal's children told her they will hold a funeral in the next couple of days.
Heartbroken and upset, Mickey returns to his hearing and surrenders himself to the police, waving his speedy trial and going into custody.
Episode 7 begins with Mickey in prison and getting a visit from Legal. However, it’s a dream and Legal jokes that he had “one too many pastrami sandwiches.”
Because he was locked up, Mickey had to miss Legal’s funeral.
“What am I going to do without you, Legal?” Mickey says, with his dear friend replying, “Same thing you did with me. Figure it out.”
Even in the dream scenario, Legal continues to give Mickey advice on his case.

title: King Charles ready to help police as they assess claims Andrew shared sensitive info with Epstein
summary: British police are looking into a claim that former Prince Andrew shared confidential trade documents with disgraced financier and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein while serving as a U.K. trade envoy
story: British police are looking into a claim that former Prince Andrew shared confidential trade documents with disgraced financier and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein while serving as a U.K. trade envoy.

Thames Valley Police, which serves the area west of London, including Windsor, one of the British royal family’s residences, said it was assessing new allegations against the younger brother of King Charles III.
“We can confirm receipt of this report and are assessing the information in line with our established procedures,” the police said in a statement.

The recent release by the Justice Department of millions of documents related to Epstein has intensified a storm that has raged around the former prince, now known simply as Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, and his relationship with the convicted sex offender.

A Buckingham Palace spokesperson said in a statement later Monday that if approached by police, the king was “ready to support them as you would expect." Charles "has made clear, in words and through unprecedented actions, his profound concern at allegations which continue to come to light in respect of Mr Mountbatten-Windsor’s conduct," the spokesperson said, adding that "Their Majesties’ thoughts and sympathies have been, and remain with, the victims of any and all forms of abuse.”

Prince William and Princess Kate also spoke out. “I can confirm The Prince and Princess have been deeply concerned by the continuing revelations. Their thoughts remain focused on the victims,” a Kensington Palace spokesperson told journalists Monday.

According to Republic, an anti-monarchy organization, it has instructed lawyers to “consider a private prosecution against Andrew over allegations of sexual assault, corruption and misconduct in public office.”
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
At the center of the latest controversy is an email exchange from Nov. 30, 2010, from the latest release of Epstein files.

The email chain, seen by NBC News, appears to show the former prince forwarding to Epstein “visit reports for Vietnam, Singapore, Hong Kong and Shenzhen” in relation to the royal's recent visit to Southeast Asia.

The email from Amit Patel, who signed as the special adviser to the then-Duke of York, appeared to contain an attached doc file and was forwarded to Epstein within five minutes of receipt, the documents show. NBC News could not confirm what exactly was in the documents attached titled VR_HONG_KONG_OCT2010_vFINAL.doc; VR_SHENZHEN_OCT2010_vFINAL.doc;VR_SINGAPORE_OCT2010_vFINAL.doc; VR_VIETNAM_OCT2010_vFINAL.doc
NBC News is reaching out to Patel for comment.
Trade envoys are usually barred from sharing sensitive or commercial documents under confidentiality rules.

The November 2010 email isn’t the only one that references Mountbatten-Windsor’s trips abroad. In a September 2010 email, David Stern, an aide to then-prince, tells Epstein that Mountbatten-Windsor was planning a trip to Vietnam, Singapore, Hong Kong and Shenzhen. Then, in October 2010, Stern messaged Epstein via Skype, “Back in hong kong from Shenzhen.” NBC News has not been able to reach Stern for comment.

Queen Elizabeth II's second son was the United Kingdom's special representative for international trade and investment from 2001 to 2011. The U.K. Department for Business and Trade told NBC News that it is ready to help police assess the allegations. He was stripped of his royal titles last year and has become estranged from the royal family over his ties to Epstein.

One of the most prominent survivors of Epstein’s sexual abuse, Virginia Roberts Giuffre, had alleged that the financier trafficked her to his powerful friends, including Andrew, against whom she filed a lawsuit in 2021 alleging that the former prince sexually abused her when she was 17.

Mountbatten-Windsor has denied having had sex with Giuffre. He stepped back from active royal duties in 2019 amid the controversy and reached a legal settlement with Giuffre for an undisclosed amount in 2022.
Mountbatten-Windsor has always denied any wrongdoing in relation to his affiliation with Epstein. NBC News has reached out to his office for comment but did not immediately receive a response.

title: Why the Epstein scandal is Keir Starmer's most perilous moment yet after chief of staff resigns
summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer is facing what could be the most precarious moment of his time as Britain’s prime minister, as the global scandal surrounding Jeffrey Epstein envelops his government
story: LONDON — Keir Starmer is facing what could be the most precarious moment of his time as Britain’s prime minister, as the global scandal surrounding Jeffrey Epstein envelops his government.

Weakened by record-low approval ratings, policy U-turns and cost-of-living pressures, and with support bleeding to far-right challengers, Starmer’s leadership was already under heavy strain, but analysts say the latest revelations could further undermine his authority and raise serious questions about his judgment.

Peter Mandelson, a longtime political grandee, is facing a police investigation over his ties to the late Epstein, including allegations he shared sensitive information with the convicted sex offender while serving as a Cabinet minister nearly two decades ago.

Starmer said last week that he knew about Mandelson’s relationship with Epstein when he nominated him as ambassador to the United States in December 2024, but that Mandelson had “lied repeatedly” about the extent of his past contact with the disgraced financier.
Starmer apologized to victims for “believing Mandelson’s lies” and said the former minister had “betrayed our country” in his dealings with Epstein.
The prime minister's chief of staff and longtime aide, Morgan McSweeney, resigned Sunday, saying the "decision to appoint Peter Mandelson was wrong."

“I advised the Prime Minister to make that appointment and I take full responsibility for that advice,” McSweeney said in a statement. He said Mandelson has “damaged our party, our country and trust in politics itself.”
Starmer said Sunday that he owed McSweeney "a debt of gratitude" and thanked him for his work in government, in a statement that did not mention Mandelson.

Police searched Mandelson’s two homes on Friday. The searches were “related to an ongoing investigation into misconduct in public office offences,” a Metropolitan Police statement said. No arrests were made, and in line with British policing convention, Mandelson was not named in the statement.

The scandal has turned up the pressure on Starmer as the controversy unsettles his party and threatens to shatter his already fragile promise of a steady, scandal-free government. A little over 18 months since his Labour government won a landslide victory, analysts say Starmer is now fighting for his political future.
“It seems yet another example of Starmer’s lack of political judgment and poor decision-making,” Peter Dorey, a professor of politics at Cardiff University, told NBC News. “His most serious yet.”

Conservative opposition leader Kemi Badenoch has called Starmer’s position “untenable,” while the centrist Liberal Democrats have called for a confidence vote to test the support of Starmer’s Labour lawmakers, some of whom have questioned whether he can remain in office.

A small number of Labour MPs, largely existing critics of Starmer, have called on him to go. Neil Duncan-Jordan told the BBC on Friday that a “reset” requires “changing who’s in charge,” while Rachael Maskell told the broadcaster it was “inevitable” he would have to quit.

Mandelson was dismissed as the U.K.’s ambassador to the United States in September after revelations about his friendship with Epstein emerged. Last Sunday, he stepped down from the party after the latest release of files by the U.S. Department of Justice.
But documents continue to reveal the full extent of Mandelson’s relationship with Epstein, showing a man who lobbied his own government while alerting Epstein to key upcoming decisions.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

“Trying hard to amend,” Mandelson, then serving as the business secretary, wrote to Epstein in December 2009 about a planned tax on bankers’ bonuses, according to the released documents. “Treasury digging in but I am on [the] case.”

He also appears to have leaked an internal economics briefing about assets the government believed it could sell. Mandelson forwarded it to Epstein with the added line: “Interesting note that’s gone to the PM.”

Mandelson has previously denied knowledge of Epstein’s crimes and denied any wrongdoing connected to him. In a statement last week he repeated an apology “to the women and girls whose voices should have been heard long before now.” His representatives did not respond to a request for further comment.

Starmer has promised to release files relating to Mandelson’s appointment as U.S. ambassador, which he says will prove Mandelson lied about the full extent of his friendship with Epstein when he was being vetted for the role.
But critics say Starmer’s decision to push on with Mandelson’s appointment, with even partial knowledge about his relationship with Epstein, raises serious questions about his decision-making.

“Either Starmer failed to have Mandelson fully ‘vetted’ before appointing him, or Starmer knew of Mandelson’s former association with Epstein, but appointed him anyway,” said Dorey. “Either would constitute a serious error of judgment by Starmer.”
The saga has proved particularly bruising given Starmer’s promise of steady, scandal-free government after numerous corruption scandals dogged the previous Conservative government.

The prime minister “was chosen by his party and by voters because he supposedly personified probity and competence,” said Tim Bale, a professor of politics at Queen Mary University of London. “After this, he can’t even claim those qualities.”

Mandelson was a controversial figure before his appointment as ambassador, having twice resigned from government during the last Labour administration over breaches of the ministerial code and accusations of financial misconduct.

Despite that, Mandelson was chosen for the U.K.’s most high-profile diplomatic role following Donald Trump’s election in part because of “his background and his relationships with very rich people,” Jamie Gaskarth, a professor of foreign policy and international relations at the Open University, told NBC News.
“It paid off initially with the friendly relations between Starmer and President Trump,” he added, “but in the long term, those kind of character issues have come back to haunt him.”

Olivia O’Sullivan, the U.K. director in the World Programme at Chatham House, a London-based think tank, echoed Gaskarth’s comments, noting that Starmer’s government had appointed somebody “they thought could handle that universe” in Mandelson.
“That has ended up kind of backfiring, really, in their faces,” she added.
Despite the bruising scandal, Starmer is unlikely to be toppled immediately, according to experts, with his main potential successors all facing difficulties that may hinder a leadership challenge.

Health Secretary Wes Streeting is facing scrutiny over his own past ties to Mandelson, while Starmer’s former deputy Angela Rayner is still the subject of an investigation into her tax affairs. Manchester’s popular Labour Mayor Andy Burnham was recently denied the opportunity to run for Parliament, making it impossible for him to challenge Starmer.

“A lot of the obvious replacements for Starmer find themselves in quite tricky positions,” said Andrew Barclay, a politics lecturer at the University of Sheffield. This contributes to a “false sense of security” for the prime minister, he added.
With local elections looming in May, where Labour is widely expected to perform poorly, Starmer’s possible rivals may not be keen to trigger an immediate leadership contest.
“There are several kinds of structural things that are keeping him in place right now,” added Barclay, “but whether that will save his premiership in the long term, I am incredibly doubtful.”
Dorey at Cardiff University agreed, saying “a lack of better alternatives” would keep him safe for now.
“Starmer is likely to remain as Labour leader and prime minister for the foreseeable future — but with his political authority and credibility seriously weakened,” he said.

title: Some Iranians rally behind the regime, but public anger runs deep after protest crackdown
summary: Supporters of Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, rallied to support him in Tehran this walks as talks took place with the U.S.
story: A heavy security presence accompanied a crowd gathered in a square in downtown Tehran earlier this week. The streets around them were closed.

But unlike a month ago, when anti-government protests broke out across the country, this crowd carried pictures of the country’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, footage taken Wednesday showed. Some waved Iranian flags, children had their faces painted, and loud music played — something that was once outlawed by the country’s hard-line ruling clerics.

Technically, the crowds gathered in Valiasr Square were there to celebrate the birth of Imam Mahdi, a prominent figure in Islam, prophesied to appear near the end of times and fill the world with peace, supported by Prophet Isa, as Jesus is known in Islam.
Politics was not far from the surface. Among the mostly religious songs and chants, the crowd shouted messages supportive of the government and, at one point, “Death to America.”

Nearby, kiosks flew flags bearing the logo of Hezbollah, the Tehran backed militant group in Lebanon, and took pictures in a cardboard cutout featuring its former leader Hasan Nasrallah, who was killed by Israel in September 2024.

Traditionally attended by thousands of government workers and supporters of the theocratic regime, which has been in place since 1979, the Mid-Shab’an Eid gathering appeared to be much smaller this year, perhaps a reflection of the shock many are feeling in the wake of last month’s protests and the bloody crackdown that followed.

Almost 7,000 were killed in demonstrations across the country including in Tehran, the U.S.-based Human Rights Activists News Agency said Thursday. The group, which says that it verifies each death with a network of activists on the ground in Iran and that its data goes through “multiple internal checks,” believes many more were likely killed. More than 50,000 have been arrested, the group says.

The scale of the protests and their intensity was “unprecedented,” according to Anoush Ehteshami, a professor of international relations at Durham University in the U.K. and the author of multiple books about the Middle East and Iran.
While it is hard to gauge the popularity of the regime, he told NBC News in a telephone interview Thursday that “there is massive unrest and unhappiness” among large swaths of the population.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

People may be “cowed into silence for the moment,” he said, but that doesn’t change the reality for a regime that is isolated internationally and hit hard by sanctions imposed by the U.S. and other countries over its nuclear program and human rights abuses.

“It’s getting hammered in all directions, and of course its currency is falling off trees, absolutely worthless,” he said, referring to the country’s dire economic situation, which has seen the rial hit record lows and inflation rates soar, leaving many struggling with the cost of living and rising prices for everyday goods.

Nonetheless, he said, an “increasingly small number stand to still gain from the regime,” including government workers and members of the security establishment, as well as their families, which runs into hundreds of thousands of people in a population of 90 million.

He added that the economy “is controlled by a cabal attached to the state machinery,” some of whom profit from the international sanctions. “Generally, they control trade, and control of trade gives them a monopoly,” he said. “A lot of these guys don’t actually want sanctions to be lifted.”

Talking to people inside Iran, no one really thinks things can return to the way they were before the protests, according to Trita Parsi, the executive vice president of the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft, a Washington-based think tank. “It just cannot happen, not after this level of killing,” he added.
The fact that some Iranians had called for foreign military intervention was unprecedented, he said in a telephone interview Friday.
Some of those pleas came after President Donald Trump threatened military action to back up his promise to help protesters.

The U.S. military has moved aircraft and land-based air defense systems into the Middle East, while the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier and its attendant ships are getting closer to being within striking distance of Tehran. But American and Iranian officials met Friday for talks to try to avoid a war, according to two U.S. officials.

Parsi said there had been “significant rallying around the flag” and a “huge surge of nationalism” after Israel and the U.S. attacked nuclear sites in the country in June. “That can happen again,” added Parsi, the author of three books about Iran. “We have plenty of data to see how air wars tend to push populations in the direction of nationalism, unity and battling around the flag.”
In Tehran, most are trying to push on with their lives. In the past, throngs of people would have voluntarily organized celebrations for Imam Mahdi. On Wednesday, it was organized by the government.

title: Key U.S.-Russia treaty expires, ushering in an era without caps on nuclear weapons
summary: The last major nuclear arms control treaty between Russia and the United States expires Thursday, ushering in an era without caps set by the world’s largest nuclear powers
story: The last major nuclear arms control treaty between Russia and the United States expires Thursday, ushering in an era without caps set by the world’s largest nuclear powers.

Without the New START treaty, which caps the number of deployed nuclear warheads at 1,550 on each side, there will be no limits on the American and Russian arsenals. Not only are there no discussions between Washington and Moscow on what comes next, but also officials from both countries are left guessing about the other side’s capabilities and intentions, increasing the possibility of misunderstandings and an unrestricted nuclear arms race not seen since the 1960s, experts and officials warn.

“For the first time in more than half a century, we face a world without any binding limits on the strategic nuclear arsenals” of Russia and the U.S., United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres said in a statement, calling it a “grave moment” for international peace and security.

President Donald Trump referred to the treaty Thursday on Truth Social as a “badly negotiated deal” and said that rather than extend it, “we should have our Nuclear Experts work on a new, improved, and modernized Treaty.”

The U.S. and Russia possess nearly 90% of all nuclear weapons, or more than 10,500 warheads combined, according to the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute’s 2025 assessment. China’s nuclear arsenal currently stands at 600 warheads, but is growing faster than any other country’s, by about 100 new warheads a year since 2023, the report said. Together, the three powers’ nuclear arsenals would wipe out life on Earth several times over.

“The Cold War is full of examples where each side had preconceptions and assumptions about what the other side was doing, some of which was faulty and which led to expensive competitions on who was seen to be ahead or behind,” said Mike Albertson, who was involved in negotiating, ratifying and implementing New START.

title: 9 dead in shootings at school and home in British Columbia
summary: Seven people were shot dead at a school in British Columbia on Tuesday, while two others were found dead at a residence believed to be connected to the campus shooting, police said.
story: Seven people were shot dead at a school in British Columbia on Tuesday, while two others were found dead at a residence believed to be connected to the campus shooting, police said.
